title: Series title on Season page is pulling from TVdb name, not local database.

On a Season page for a Show, the top shows "[Show Title]" on one line followed by the Season name/number. However, if the show title is different from the one used on TheTVdb, [Show Title] on the Season page uses TheTVdb title instead of the user's local setting.

title: MusicArtist entries appear in search for users without access to the containing music library
author: spongybivalve491
body:
### Description of the bug

A user account that has been denied access to a Music library (via Users → Access → "Enable access to all libraries" unchecked, Music not selected) can no longer see the music library, albums, or tracks — as expected. However, MusicArtist entities (e.g. "Dire Straits") still appear in that user's global search results.

Clicking the artist result loads indefinitely and never resolves, because the underlying albums/tracks are correctly access-blocked. So the media itself is properly walled off — this is a cosmetic/metadata leak in search, not an access-control hole, but it exposes artist names the user shouldn't see and produces a dead, forever-loading result.

### Reproduction steps

Have a Music library and at least two user accounts (one admin "A", one restricted "B").
As admin, go to Users → B → Access, uncheck Enable access to all libraries, and do not tick the Music library. Save.
Log in as user B.
Confirm the Music library, its albums, and tracks are all hidden from home/browse — they are.
Search for an artist name that exists only in that Music library

### What is the current _bug_ behavior?

The artist appears in search results. Selecting it loads forever and never opens, since the underlying media is (correctly) inaccessible

### What is the expected _correct_ behavior?

No MusicArtist results appear, since user B has no access to any library containing that artist.

### Jellyfin Server version

10.11.11

title: Live TV: one slow/stalled tuner `Open()` freezes ALL Live TV for 100s (global lock held across the network connect); the shared-stream path has no connect timeout and leaks on failure
author: luuvasara
body:
### Environment - **Jellyfin:** 10.11.11 (official, Docker/Linux) - **Live TV source:** M3U tuner, `AllowStreamSharing = true`, `TunerCount = 0` (unlimited), pointed at an infinite HTTP MPEG-TS live source (an IPTV/restreamer feed). --- ### Impact (summary) A single live channel whose upstream `Open()` is slow to first byte does **not** just fail itself — because that `Open()` runs inside a **single process-global lock** and has **no connect deadline** (only the framework-default 100 s `HttpClient.Timeout`), it **serializes and blocks every other user's Live TV open _and_ close, server-wide, for up to 100 s.** The originating tune then dies with `LiveTvConflictException: Unable to find host to play channel`, and the abandoned open leaks its reader connection. This is, I believe, a common root cause behind the recurring "Live TV randomly won't start / everything just spins for a while" reports. --- ### Why this matters specifically for stream sharing (and why the workaround isn't a real fix) We run multiple household clients that frequently watch the **same** live channel. `AllowStreamSharing = true` is exactly what makes that viable: Jellyfin opens **one** upstream connection (`SharedHttpStream`) and fans it out to all viewers. This is essential when the upstream provider **caps concurrent connections** (ours allows only a couple) and to avoid pulling the same high-bitrate feed N times. The only known workaround — `AllowStreamSharing = false` — makes Jellyfin fall back to a plain `LiveStream` where **each client opens its own upstream connection**. For a connection-capped provider that immediately exceeds the cap and defeats the entire purpose of sharing. So the bug lives precisely in the path we (and anyone with a capped/expensive source) must use. **We're asking for the shared path to be fixed, not for sharing to be avoided.** --- ### When it triggers (circumstantial dependencies) All of the following together — none of them exotic for IPTV: 1. **Stream sharing on** (`AllowStreamSharing = true`) → the `SharedHttpStream` path is used (vs. plain `LiveStream`). 2. **Infinite live HTTP source** (Live TV, not a finite file). 3. **The source is momentarily slow to send the first byte / headers.** It does **not** need to be permanently dead. Everyday IPTV causes: provider reconnect-throttle, per-channel concurrency cap briefly refusing a second connect, an upstream node restarting, a transient network blip. A short stall blocks other users for that duration; a full stall hits the **100 s** ceiling. 4. **Any concurrent Live TV activity** during that window — another client tuning, a scheduled recording starting, a guide-triggered open — is blocked behind the global lock, so the failure appears to affect *unrelated* channels/users. --- ### Reproduction (deterministic, synthetic origin — no external services) A ~120-line stdlib-Python origin that logs each connection's lifecycle, reachable directly by the Jellyfin server (bridge-direct, no reverse proxy), serving synthetic null-TS. It exposes: - `/instant` — headers + body immediately (control); - `/stall-headers?s=120` — accepts, never sends headers; - `/stall-body?s=120` — headers immediately, first body byte after 120 s. Add it as an M3U tuner with `AllowStreamSharing = true`, then: | Channel | Result (measured, 10.11.11) | |---|---| | `/instant` | `M3UTunerHost: Live stream opened after 3.78ms`, streams — normal open is instant. | | `/stall-headers` | Open dies at **exactly 100.0 s**; origin log shows it *never errored* and Jellyfin dropped the socket at 100 s → `TaskCanceledException: HttpClient.Timeout of 100 seconds elapsing` → `LiveTvConflictException`. | | `/stall-body` | `Live stream opened after 120121ms` — Jellyfin waited the **full 120 s** and did **not** cancel at 100 s. So the 100 s bounds the *header* wait, **not** the body read. | | **Cascade** | While a stall channel's `Open()` is in flight, tuning *any other* channel blocks until the stall resolves/times out. | Observed error (production, from the real feed): ``` M3UTunerHost: Opening "SharedHttpStream" Live stream from "http:///" (exactly 100.0s later) [ERR] M3UTunerHost: Error opening tuner System.Threading.Tasks.TaskCanceledException: The request was canceled due to the configured HttpClient.Timeout of 100 seconds elapsing. ---> IOException: Unable to read data from the transport connection: Operation canceled at Jellyfin.LiveTv.TunerHosts.SharedHttpStream.Open(CancellationToken) at Jellyfin.LiveTv.TunerHosts.BaseTunerHost.GetChannelStream(...) LiveTvConflictException: Unable to find host to play channel ``` (The full harness `repro_origin.py` + runbook can be attached / linked.) --- ### Root cause (v10.11.11 source) **1 — `Open()` has no connect/first-byte deadline.** [`SharedHttpStream.cs:44`](https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/blob/v10.11.11/src/Jellyfin.LiveTv/TunerHosts/SharedHttpStream.cs#L44): the `openCancellationToken` parameter is never used, and the `GetAsync` at [L58-60](https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/blob/v10.11.11/src/Jellyfin.LiveTv/TunerHosts/SharedHttpStream.cs#L58-L60) is issued with `CancellationToken.None`. `NamedClient.Default` never sets `.Timeout` ([`Startup.cs:97-104`](https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/blob/v10.11.11/Jellyfin.Server/Startup.cs#L97-L104)), so the only bound is the CLR's 100 s default. (Measured: this bounds the header wait, not the body read.) **2 — a thrown `Open()` is never disposed → leak.** [`BaseTunerHost.cs:216-219`](https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/blob/v10.11.11/src/Jellyfin.LiveTv/TunerHosts/BaseTunerHost.cs#L216-L219): the catch only logs; the `SharedHttpStream` whose `Open()` threw is not `Close()`/`Dispose()`d, so its `LiveStreamCancellationTokenSource` is never cancelled and the fire-and-forget copy loop + upstream connection stay open. **3 — the network `Open()` runs inside a process-global lock (the blast radius).** [`MediaSourceManager.cs:60`](https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/blob/v10.11.11/Emby.Server.Implementations/Library/MediaSourceManager.cs#L60): `private readonly AsyncNonKeyedLocker _liveStreamLocker = new(1);` — a single, **non-keyed** mutex on the **singleton** `MediaSourceManager` ([`ApplicationHost.cs:542`](https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/blob/v10.11.11/Emby.Server.Implementations/ApplicationHost.cs#L542)). [`OpenLiveStreamInternal` (`:499-523`)](https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/blob/v10.11.11/Emby.Server.Implementations/Library/MediaSourceManager.cs#L499-L523) acquires it and holds it across the unbroken `await` chain `provider.OpenMediaSource → DefaultLiveTvService.GetChannelStreamWithDirectStreamProvider → BaseTunerHost.GetChannelStream → liveStream.Open()` — i.e. across the real network connect. [`CloseLiveStream` (`:861-884`)](https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/blob/v10.11.11/Emby.Server.Implementations/Library/MediaSourceManager.cs#L861-L884) takes the same lock, so a stalled open blocks closes too. **4 — related, already fixed on master:** abandoned-before-playback streams aren't closed (`SessionManager._activeLiveStreamSessions` only populated on `OnPlaybackStart/Progress`); addressed by #17178, which is **not** in 10.11.11 and does not touch defects 1–3. --- ### Suggested fix (impact order) 1. **Bound the connect** — `SharedHttpStream.Open()`: link `openCancellationToken` with an explicit short connect/first-byte deadline instead of `CancellationToken.None`. (A shorter `HttpClient.Timeout` alone is insufficient — it doesn't bound the body read.) 2. **Don't hold the global lock across the network connect** — in `OpenLiveStreamInternal`, take `_liveStreamLocker` only to guard the `_openStreams` mutation; perform `provider.OpenMediaSource(...)`/`Open()` outside it. This removes the cross-user head-of-line blocking even when a source is slow. 3. **Dispose a failed open** — in `BaseTunerHost.GetChannelStream`'s catch, `await liveStream.Close()`/`Dispose()` before continuing. 4. Ship/backport **#17178** for the abandoned-session close gap. Fixes 1 + 2 are the important pair: 1 caps how long any single stall lasts; 2 stops one stall from affecting everyone. --- ### Workaround (for others hitting this on 10.11.x) Set `AllowStreamSharing = false` on the tuner — `M3UTunerHost.GetChannelStream` then returns a plain `LiveStream` whose `Open()` is a no-op, bypassing `SharedHttpStream` and the global-lock stall entirely. **Caveat:** this disables upstream stream sharing, so each client opens its own connection — not viable if your source caps concurrent connections (the exact reason to want sharing in the first place).
Full reproduction harness — repro_origin.py (stdlib only, no deps; click to expand) ```python #!/usr/bin/env python3 """ Instrumented origin server for reproducing Jellyfin 10.11.11 Live TV SharedHttpStream.Open() defects in a clean room (no docker-proxy, no real provider, no infra fingerprints). Stdlib only. Every TCP connection is logged with monotonic timestamps for: accept, response headers sent, first body byte sent, periodic byte counts, and close (with who closed and after how long). That server-side lifecycle log is the hard proof: it shows exactly what the origin did while Jellyfin's Open() sat for 100s. Endpoints (point a Jellyfin M3U tuner at these): /instant 200 immediately, then infinite MPEG-TS null packets ~2 Mbit. CONTROL: Open() should resolve fast; also the base for the leak test (play then abandon -> watch the connection linger). /stall-headers?s=120 accept, hold the response (no headers) for s seconds, then stream. Proves Defect 1: Open()'s GetAsync waits for headers and is bounded ONLY by the framework-default 100s HttpClient timeout -> TaskCanceledException at ~100s even though the socket is alive and the server never errored. /drip?s=30 200 immediately, then one TS packet every s seconds. Proves the 100s client timeout bounds the BODY read too (not just headers): headers arrive instantly yet Open() still cancels at ~100s because first-copy/Resolve never completes in time. Usage: python3 repro_origin.py [bind_host] [port] (default 0.0.0.0:8888) """ import socket, sys, time, threading, itertools BIND = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "0.0.0.0" PORT = int(sys.argv[2]) if len(sys.argv) > 2 else 8888 T0 = time.monotonic() _ids = itertools.count(1) _lock = threading.Lock() def log(cid, peer, event, extra=""): with _lock: print("[%8.3f] conn=%-4s %-21s %-16s %s" % (time.monotonic() - T0, cid, peer, event, extra), flush=True) # One 188-byte MPEG-TS null packet (PID 0x1FFF), sync byte 0x47. Not decodable # content -- irrelevant: Open()/StartStreaming resolve on first copied bytes, not # on a valid decode, so null packets suffice to prove open/leak/timeout behavior. NULL_TS = bytes([0x47, 0x1F, 0xFF, 0x10]) + b"\xff" * 184 def parse_qs(path): q = {} if "?" in path: path, qs = path.split("?", 1) for kv in qs.split("&"): if "=" in kv: k, v = kv.split("=", 1); q[k] = v return path, q def send_headers(conn): conn.sendall(b"HTTP/1.1 200 OK\r\nContent-Type: video/mp2t\r\n" b"Connection: close\r\nCache-Control: no-cache\r\n\r\n") def handle(conn, addr): cid = str(next(_ids)); peer = "%s:%d" % addr conn.settimeout(300) try: req = conn.recv(4096).decode("latin1", "replace") line = req.split("\r\n", 1)[0] method, path = (line.split(" ") + ["", ""])[:2] raw_path, q = parse_qs(path) log(cid, peer, "ACCEPT", "%s %s" % (method, path)) if raw_path == "/playlist.m3u": host = q.get("host", addr[0]) # advertise ourselves; override with ?host=repro-origin base = "http://%s:%d" % (host, PORT) m3u = ("#EXTM3U\n" "#EXTINF:-1 tvg-id=\"repro.instant\",REPRO INSTANT\n%s/instant\n" "#EXTINF:-1 tvg-id=\"repro.stallhdr\",REPRO STALL-HEADERS\n%s/stall-headers?s=120\n" "#EXTINF:-1 tvg-id=\"repro.stallbody\",REPRO STALL-BODY\n%s/stall-body?s=120\n" % (base, base, base)) body = m3u.encode() conn.sendall(b"HTTP/1.1 200 OK\r\nContent-Type: application/x-mpegurl\r\n" b"Content-Length: %d\r\nConnection: close\r\n\r\n" % len(body) + body) log(cid, peer, "PLAYLIST_SERVED", "%d channels" % 3) return if raw_path == "/stall-headers": s = int(q.get("s", "120")) log(cid, peer, "HOLDING_HEADERS", "will send headers after %ss (>100s)" % s) end = time.monotonic() + s while time.monotonic() < end: # touch the socket so we notice when the client (Jellyfin) gives up conn.settimeout(1.0) try: if conn.recv(1, socket.MSG_PEEK) == b"": log(cid, peer, "CLIENT_CLOSED", "during header-hold at %.1fs (Jellyfin's 100s timeout)" % (time.monotonic() - (end - s))) return except socket.timeout: pass except OSError: log(cid, peer, "CLIENT_RESET", "during header-hold at %.1fs" % (time.monotonic() - (end - s))) return send_headers(conn); log(cid, peer, "HEADERS_SENT", "(after the hold)") stream_ts(conn, cid, peer) elif raw_path == "/stall-body": # headers immediately, but hold the FIRST BODY BYTE for s>100s. Discriminates # whether the 100s HttpClient.Timeout bounds the content read too (source claim) # or only the header wait: if Open() dies at ~100s the body read IS bounded. s = int(q.get("s", "120")) send_headers(conn); log(cid, peer, "HEADERS_SENT", "immediately; holding FIRST BODY BYTE for %ss (>100s)" % s) end = time.monotonic() + s while time.monotonic() < end: conn.settimeout(1.0) try: if conn.recv(1, socket.MSG_PEEK) == b"": log(cid, peer, "CLIENT_CLOSED", "during body-hold at %.1fs (client gave up before any body byte)" % (s - (end - time.monotonic()))) return except socket.timeout: pass except OSError: log(cid, peer, "CLIENT_RESET", "during body-hold"); return log(cid, peer, "FIRST_BYTE", "after %ss body-hold (client waited it out)" % s) stream_ts(conn, cid, peer) else: # /instant (and default): headers now, then continuous stream send_headers(conn); log(cid, peer, "HEADERS_SENT", "immediately") stream_ts(conn, cid, peer) except (BrokenPipeError, ConnectionResetError): log(cid, peer, "CLIENT_CLOSED", "socket broken at %.1fs (client went away)" % (time.monotonic() - T0)) except Exception as e: log(cid, peer, "ERROR", repr(e)) finally: try: conn.close() except OSError: pass def stream_ts(conn, cid, peer): # ~2 Mbit: 166 null packets (~31KB) every ~0.125s burst = NULL_TS * 166 sent = 0; t = time.monotonic() try: while True: conn.sendall(burst); sent += len(burst) if sent == len(burst): log(cid, peer, "FIRST_BYTE", "streaming ~2Mbit null-TS") now = time.monotonic() if now - t >= 10: log(cid, peer, "STREAMING", "%d MB sent, %.0fs elapsed" % (sent // (1<<20), now - T0)); t = now time.sleep(0.125) except (BrokenPipeError, ConnectionResetError, OSError): log(cid, peer, "CLIENT_CLOSED", "%d MB in, closed at %.1fs" % (sent // (1<<20), time.monotonic() - T0)) def main(): srv = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) srv.setsockopt(socket.SOL_SOCKET, socket.SO_REUSEADDR, 1) srv.bind((BIND, PORT)); srv.listen(64) log("-", "-", "LISTENING", "%s:%d endpoints: /instant /stall-headers?s=120 /stall-body?s=120" % (BIND, PORT)) while True: conn, addr = srv.accept() threading.Thread(target=handle, args=(conn, addr), daemon=True).start() if __name__ == "__main__": main() ```
The full reproduction harness is inlined above; happy to provide any further logs.

title: Jellyfin not selecting the forced subtitle when the subtitle mode is "Always Play" and the audio and preferred subtitle languages do not match
author: mschumacher69
body:
### Description of the bug

Currently, when the subtitle mode is set to "Always Play", it selects the non-forced subtitle by default. While this is OK when the audio and preferred subtitle language match, because in this case, the forced subtitle would only be a translation for a few lines in a different language instead of captioning the whole audio, this shouldn't be the behavior when the audio and preferred subtitle language do not match because in this case, the forced subtitle would be a translation for the whole audio and should be selected by default.

For example, if the audio language is Spanish, the English subtitle would be set to Default and Forced in most cases because it would be a translation for the whole audio. But if the subtitle mode is set to "Always Play", it is not selecting the default English subtitle because it is set to Forced, and it selects the SDH subtitle instead. This shouldn't be the behavior because when the preferred subtitle language does not match the audio language, it should select the Forced subtitle (if it matches the preferred subtitle language), not the SDH subtitle.

### Reproduction steps

1. Set Preferred subtitle language to English
2. Set Subtitle Mode to Always Play
3. Play a video with a non-english audio and an english subtitle that is set to Forced (and Default in most cases) and another SDH english subtitle.

### What is the current _bug_ behavior?

title: Live TV: abandoned M3U tuner streams are never released, exhausting the tuner limit ("simultaneous stream limit has been reached")
author: dwertle
body:
### Please describe your bug

When a Live TV client stops playback or changes channels without an explicit stream close, Jellyfin's M3U tuner host never releases the live stream. The tuner slot stays consumed even though no `ffmpeg` is running and no session is playing. Once `TunerCount` such orphaned streams accumulate, **all** Live TV fails to open with:

```
[ERR] Jellyfin.LiveTv.TunerHosts.M3UTunerHost: Error opening tuner
MediaBrowser.Controller.LiveTv.LiveTvConflictException: M3U simultaneous stream limit has been reached.
MediaBrowser.Controller.LiveTv.LiveTvConflictException: Unable to find host to play channel
```

At that point `GET /Sessions` shows nothing playing and there are no `ffmpeg` child processes — the slots are held by "ghost" streams. Only restarting the server clears them.

This is especially easy to hit by channel-surfing: each channel change that isn't cleanly closed leaks one slot, so a tuner with a low `TunerCount` wedges after only a few channel changes.

### Reproduction Steps

1. Configure an M3U tuner with `TunerCount = N` (e.g. 2).
2. Play a live channel, then change channel (or close the client) `N` times without an explicit stop.
3. Observe that each leaves a live stream registered but with no running `ffmpeg`.
4. Attempt playback again → it fails with `M3U simultaneous stream limit has been reached`, while `/Sessions` shows nothing playing.
5. The only way to recover is restarting Jellyfin.

### Jellyfin Version

10.11.11

### Environment

```markdown
- OS: Ubuntu (Linux 6.8.0)
- Clients observed: Jellyfin Web + third-party clients
- Playback Method: Live TV via M3U tuner + XMLTV
- TunerCount: reproduced at 2 (still reachable at higher counts by surfing more channels)
```

### Additional context

Clients don't reliably send a live-stream close (`POST /LiveStreams/Close`, or `onPlaybackStopped` with the `LiveStreamId`) on channel change / abandonment, so the streams leak. But beyond the clients, the **server** does not appear to reap orphaned/idle live streams, so a single client that abandons a stream can permanently wedge the tuner until a restart. It would help if the M3U tuner host released streams that have no active consumer (no `ffmpeg` / no keep-alive) after a timeout, so one leaked stream can't block all Live TV.

title: AMD VAAPI Vulkan tonemapping incorrectly fails on RHEL kernels due to kernel version check.
author: meatzie
body:
### Description of the bug

The AMD VAAPI + Vulkan/libplacebo tonemapping code path is gated by a kernel version check in `EncodingHelper.cs` (line 72) that requires kernel >= 5.15:

```csharp
private readonly Version _minKernelVersionAmdVkFmtModifier = new Version(5, 15);
```

This check was introduced in [PR #7708](https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/pull/7708) by @nyanmisaka because upstream kernel 5.15 added DRM changes required for the `VK_EXT_image_drm_format_modifier` Vulkan extension on AMD GPUs. The check is correct for most kernels since a vanilla 5.14 kernel genuinely lacks this support, so simply lowering the threshold to 5.14 would be wrong.

However, the check breaks on Linux distributions that backport kernel features. RHEL 9, CentOS Stream 9, Rocky Linux 9, AlmaLinux 9, and Red Hat CoreOS (RHCOS) all ship a kernel which reports `5.14.x` because that's what they're branched from, but they often backport features from upstream which makes the kernel version alone hard to use as a check. The RHEL 9.6 kernel (`5.14.0-570.x`) includes the relevant DRM changes from upstream 5.15+, and the `VK_EXT_image_drm_format_modifier` extension works correctly.

Because `5.14 < 5.15`, Jellyfin skips the Vulkan/libplacebo tonemapping path (line 5068) and falls back to OpenCL tonemapping (line 1090). Then the OpenCL path ends up failing because the official Docker image does not include an OpenCL runtime (ROCm or Mesa rusticl). The result is that all HDR tonemapping fails on AMD GPUs when running on any RHEL-based kernel, and Jellyfin falls back to CPU-only software transcoding.

The Vulkan path works perfectly when the kernel version check is bypassed, I was able to work around the issue by testing with a `uname()` override that reports 5.15.0 (this is a terrible idea and not even a good workaround for anyone else thinking they should try it).

Jellyfin's own ffmpeg test confirms Vulkan is fully functional on these kernels:

```
$ /usr/lib/jellyfin-ffmpeg/ffmpeg -v debug -init_hw_device drm=dr:/dev/dri/renderD128 -init_hw_device vulkan@dr
[AVHWDeviceContext] GPU listing:
    0: AMD Ryzen 9 9950X 16-Core Processor (RADV RAPHAEL_MENDOCINO) (integrated) (0x13c0)
Device 0 selected: AMD Ryzen 9 9950X 16-Core Processor (RADV RAPHAEL_MENDOCINO)
```

The only other reference to this I found wasn't a bug report, but a discussion (I'm sorry if that counds as a duplicate) that went unanswered [Discussion #12459](https://github.com/orgs/jellyfin/discussions/12459), where a user with an RX 5600XT reports the same OpenCL failure and wants to use Vulkan but can't.

### Reproduction steps

1. Deploy Jellyfin 10.11.11 (official Docker image `jellyfin/jellyfin`) on a host running a RHEL 9-based kernel (5.14.x) with an AMD GPU (Polaris or newer).
2. Enable VAAPI hardware acceleration in Dashboard → Playback → Transcoding.
3. Enable "Tone Mapping" in the transcoding settings.
4. Play any HDR (HEVC Main 10, bt2020/smpte2084) media file on a client that requires transcoding.


### What is the current _bug_ behavior?

FFmpeg fails immediately with:

```
[AVHWDeviceContext] Failed to get number of OpenCL platforms: -1001.
Device creation failed: -19.
Failed to set value 'opencl=ocl:.0,device_vendor=Advanced Micro Devices' for option 'init_hw_device': No such device
Error parsing global options: No such device
```

Jellyfin generates an ffmpeg command using the OpenCL tonemapping path (`-init_hw_device opencl=ocl:...`, `tonemap_opencl`) instead of the Vulkan/libplacebo path. The transcode fails entirely for HDR content. When retried by the client, Jellyfin falls back to CPU-only software transcoding.

### What is the expected _correct_ behavior?

Jellyfin should use the Vulkan/libplacebo tonemapping path for AMD GPUs on RHEL-based kernels, producing an ffmpeg command like:

```
-init_hw_device drm=dr:/dev/dri/renderD128 -init_hw_device vaapi=va@dr -init_hw_device vulkan=vk@dr -filter_hw_device vk -hwaccel vaapi -hwaccel_output_format vaapi ... -codec:v:0 hevc_vaapi ... -vf "...,libplacebo=...:tonemapping=bt.2390:...,format=vulkan,hwmap=derive_device=vaapi,format=vaapi,scale_vaapi=format=nv12"
```

This is what happens when the kernel version check is bypassed, and it works correctly with full GPU-accelerated transcoding.

### Jellyfin Server version

10.11.11

### Specify commit id

_No response_

### Specify unstable release number

_No response_

### Specify version number

_No response_

### Specify the build version

10.11.11

### Environment

```markdown
- OS: Red Hat Enterprise Linux CoreOS (RHCOS) 4.21 / RHEL 9.6
- Linux Kernel: 5.14.0-570.116.1.el9_6.x86_64
- Virtualization: Container (OpenShift / Kubernetes)
- Clients: Firefox 148.0 (Linux x86_64)
- Browser: Firefox 148.0
- FFmpeg Version: 7.1.4-Jellyfin
- Playback Method: Transcode
- Hardware Acceleration: VAAPI
- CPU Model: AMD Ryzen 9 9950X 16-Core Processor
- GPU Model: AMD Radeon 780M (RDNA 3, Raphael/Mendocino integrated)
- Plugins: Playback Reporting 17.0.0.0, Reports 18.0.0.0
- Reverse Proxy: OpenShift HAProxy Router (edge TLS)
- Base URL:
- Networking: OVNKubernetes (bridge/NAT)
- Jellyfin Data Storage & Filesystem: PVC (LVM thin, XFS, SSD)
- Media Storage & Filesystem: PVC (LVM thin, XFS, HDD)
- External Integrations:
```

### Jellyfin logs

```shell
[INF] MediaBrowser.MediaEncoding.Encoder.MediaEncoder: VAAPI device /dev/dri/renderD128 is AMD GPU
[INF] MediaBrowser.MediaEncoding.Encoder.MediaEncoder: VAAPI device /dev/dri/renderD128 supports Vulkan DRM modifier
[INF] MediaBrowser.MediaEncoding.Encoder.MediaEncoder: VAAPI device /dev/dri/renderD128 supports Vulkan DRM interop


Despite detecting full Vulkan support, the kernel version check at line 5068 of `EncodingHelper.cs` prevents the Vulkan code path from being used.
```

### FFmpeg logs

```shell
Failed transcode (OpenCL path selected due to kernel check):


ffmpeg version 7.1.4-Jellyfin
amdgpu: os_same_file_description couldn't determine if two DRM fds reference the same file description.
If they do, bad things may happen!
[AVHWDeviceContext @ 0x7f97e23f1f00] Failed to get number of OpenCL platforms: -1001.
Device creation failed: -19.
Failed to set value 'opencl=ocl:.0,device_vendor=Advanced Micro Devices' for option 'init_hw_device': No such device
Error parsing global options: No such device


Working transcode (Vulkan path, after bypassing kernel check):


/usr/lib/jellyfin-ffmpeg/ffmpeg -init_hw_device drm=dr:/dev/dri/renderD128 -init_hw_device vaapi=va@dr -init_hw_device vulkan=vk@dr -filter_hw_device vk -hwaccel vaapi -hwaccel_output_format vaapi -i file:"...mkv" -codec:v:0 hevc_vaapi -vf "setparams=...,hwmap=derive_device=drm,format=drm_prime,libplacebo=upscaler=none:downscaler=none:format=bgra:tonemapping=bt.2390:peak_detect=0:color_primaries=bt709:color_trc=bt709:colorspace=bt709,format=vulkan,hwmap=derive_device=vaapi,format=vaapi,scale_vaapi=format=nv12" ...
```

### Client / Browser logs

_No response_

### Relevant screenshots or videos

_No response_

### Additional information

### Notes

The kernel version check was added for a valid reason ([PR #7708](https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/pull/7708)). 5.15 introduced the DRM changes needed for `VK_EXT_image_drm_format_modifier` on AMD. Lowering the version to 5.14 would break users on actual upstream 5.14 kernels that lack the support.

I think that the kernel version check may be redundant though. The other three conditions at [line 5065-5067](https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/blob/master/MediaBrowser.Controller/MediaEncoding/EncodingHelper.cs#L5065-L5067) already seem to verify all the necessary capabilities at runtime:

- `IsVaapiDeviceAmd` --> confirms AMD GPU
- `isVaapiVkSupported` (`IsVaapiFullSupported()` + `IsVulkanFullSupported()`) -->confirms ffmpeg has vulkan hwaccel, libplacebo, scale_vulkan, overlay_vulkan, etc.
- `IsVaapiDeviceSupportVulkanDrmInterop` --> probes the GPU for the specific Vulkan extensions (`VK_KHR_external_memory_fd`, `VK_EXT_external_memory_dma_buf`, etc.) at startup ([`MediaEncoder.cs` line 249](https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/blob/master/MediaBrowser.MediaEncoding/Encoder/MediaEncoder.cs#L249))

All three pass on these kernels, as shown in the startup logs above. The kernel version check at line 5068 is the only thing preventing the Vulkan path from being used, so maybe it could just be removed?

This would impact any users running Jellyfin with AMD GPUs on any rhel 9 based distros like CentOS, Alma, rocky, etc

### Workaround

I'm just copying another binary into an image built from the official Docker image that intercepts `uname()` via `LD_PRELOAD` to report kernel version 5.15.0. This seems to be working with full GPU-accelerated HDR tonemapping so far and hasn't broken anything else for me yet.

### Related

- [Discussion #12459: OpenCL with AMD GPU, but I want vulkan](https://github.com/orgs/jellyfin/discussions/12459) (similar logs, but no kernel version. might be the same root cause)
- Origin of the kernel check: [PR #7708: Add Vulkan filtering support for AMD VAAPI (Vega/gfx9+)](https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/pull/7708) by @nyanmisaka
- Relevant code: [`EncodingHelper.cs` line 72](https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/blob/master/MediaBrowser.Controller/MediaEncoding/EncodingHelper.cs#L72) and [line 5064-5072](https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/blob/master/MediaBrowser.Controller/MediaEncoding/EncodingHelper.cs#L5064-L5072)

### AI assistance disclosure

I was frustrated and stumped so I used an Claude Code to try and figure out what I was missing. It was used to draft some of this report, digging around the Jellyfin source code, identifying the kernel version check as the root cause, and researching the history behind PR #7708. All reproduction, testing, and verification were performed on my OpenShift cluster with the hardware described above.

title: `MediaSources` requests block/time-out on unreachable or high-latency storage due to on-access external-stream resolution (distinct path from the known 10.11 HDD-wake regressions)
author: yogi-gharat
body:
### Relationship to existing issues (please read before closing as duplicate)

This belongs to the same family as the 10.11.x "browsing touches the media filesystem" reports, but it is a **different code path** and a **different, more severe symptom**, so it isn't covered by any of them:

- **#15755** (`File.Exists`/`GetVersionInfo` in `BaseItem.cs`, PR #15263) — closed, but reported "still broken as of 10.11.8"
- **#15598** (HDD wakes when accessing item details) — closed regression
- **#15756** (dashboard storage-metrics API, PR #13888) — open

All three describe **spun-down disks being woken** (a power/latency annoyance where the request still eventually completes). This report is about the path that builds **`MediaSources`/`MediaStreams`**, which re-resolves external subtitle/attachment sidecars against the live filesystem on every request — and when storage is **unreachable or high-latency**, the entire request **hangs and times out**, so the operation *fails outright* rather than just waking a disk. A search for `MediaInfoResolver` / `GetExternalStreams` turns up no existing report of this path.

### Summary

Requests that include `Fields=MediaSources,MediaStreams` (which the official clients issue to render episode/movie lists) cause Jellyfin to re-resolve each item's **external streams** (subtitle/attachment sidecars) against the **live filesystem** on every request, rather than trusting the `MediaStreamInfos` already cached in the library DB. If the storage is slow or momentarily unreachable, the whole list request **times out**, even though all metadata needed to render the list is already cached. Every DB-backed field (titles, overviews, images, `MediaSourceCount`) serves instantly under the same conditions.

Net effect: **browsing a TV show's episode list is impossible while storage is unreachable, despite the metadata being fully cached.**

### Who this affects

Any deployment where storage isn't guaranteed instantly responsive — not just one unusual setup:

- Network mounts (SMB/NFS/CIFS) with transient latency or brief disconnects
- Cloud / object storage (rclone mounts, cold tiers) with high first-byte latency
- Spun-down / power-managed HDDs (this is the failure-mode counterpart of #15755/#15756)
- Sleeping storage hosts (e.g. an HTPC on modern standby)

### Steps to reproduce

1. TV library on a network mount, external `.srt` sidecars next to the episodes.
2. Let the library scan complete (so `MediaStreamInfos` are cached in the DB).
3. Make the storage slow/unreachable (unmount, block the host, or let a spun-down disk / sleeping host stay down).
4. Issue these and compare (all against the same sleeping/unreachable storage):

| # | Request | Result |
|---|---------|--------|
| A | `GET /Shows/{id}/Episodes?userId={u}&Fields=Overview` | **200 — ~0.09s** ✅ |
| B | `GET /Shows/{id}/Episodes?userId={u}&Fields=ItemCounts,PrimaryImageAspectRatio,MediaSourceCount,Overview` | **200 — ~0.10s** ✅ |
| C | `GET /Shows/{id}/Episodes?userId={u}&Fields=MediaSources,MediaStreams,Path` | **hangs → client timeout (25s+)** ❌ |
| D | `GET /Items?ParentId={seasonId}&userId={u}&Fields=MediaSourceCount,Overview` | **200 — ~0.17s** ✅ |
| E | `POST /Items/{episodeId}/PlaybackInfo` | 200 but **~10s** (blocks on the same resolution) |

Only the requests that build `MediaSources`/`MediaStreams` (C, E) touch the filesystem and stall. Everything DB-backed (A, B, D) is unaffected — this isolates the trigger to `MediaSources` construction, not metadata/`File.Exists` on the item itself.

### Expected behaviour

List/browse requests should render from cached data regardless of storage reachability. Building `MediaSources` for a **list** response should trust the cached `MediaStreamInfos` and not re-enumerate/re-probe external sidecars on the live filesystem. Filesystem-touching resolution should happen at playback / `PlaybackInfo` or during scans — not on every list request.

### Actual behaviour

Every `MediaSources` request re-runs external-stream resolution against the filesystem. Observed in the server log during a stalled request:

```
[ERR] ... MediaBrowser.Common.FfmpegException: ffprobe failed - streams and format are both null.
   at MediaBrowser.MediaEncoding.Encoder.MediaEncoder.GetMediaInfoInternal(...)
   at MediaBrowser.Providers.MediaInfo.MediaInfoResolver.GetExternalStreamsAsync(
        Video video, Int32 startIndex, IDirectoryService directoryService,
        Boolean clearCache, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
```

Because a season list resolves this per episode, the stalls accumulate and the whole request times out. The cached `MediaStreamInfos` rows (populated by the last scan, including external subtitle streams) are not trusted for the response.

### Proposed fix / options

1. **Trust cached `MediaStreamInfos` for list/browse responses** — resolve external streams lazily at playback / `PlaybackInfo`, or during scheduled scans / realtime-monitor events.
2. **A per-library option** to disable on-access external-stream re-resolution (e.g. "Only detect external subtitles during library scans"), analogous to opt-in realtime monitoring.
3. **Fail fast + degrade gracefully** — if external-stream resolution can't reach the file within a short bound, return the list from cached data instead of hanging the whole request.

Any of these would also reduce the disk-wake pressure described in #15755/#15756 for the `MediaSources` path specifically.

### Environment

- **Jellyfin server:** 10.11.11 (Linux)
- **Storage:** media on SMB3/CIFS mounts (soft mounts) to a host that is not always instantly reachable
- **Library:** TV shows with ~1900 external `.srt` sidecars next to the episodes; realtime monitoring **off**, scheduled scans **off**
- **Clients:** reproduced via direct API calls; symptom observed in official clients that request `MediaSources` on list views

### Notes

There's a working **workaround** for my case (I wake the storage host on browse), so this isn't blocking me — but it exposes that metadata browsing is unnecessarily coupled to live storage access via the `MediaSources` path, distinct from the `File.Exists`/storage-API paths already tracked. Happy to provide a `--Debug` trace or test a change.

title: Successful login immediately reverts the user's LastLoginDate (stale entity overwrite in LogSessionActivity)
author: ElianCodes
body:
### This issue respects the following points:

- [x] This is a **bug**, not a question or a configuration issue.
- [x] This issue is **not** already reported on GitHub _(I've searched it)_.
- [x] I'm using an up to date version of Jellyfin Server stable (10.11.11), and I verified the code path is unchanged on master.
- [x] I agree to follow Jellyfin's Code of Conduct.
- [x] This report addresses only a single issue.

### Description of the bug

A user's `LastLoginDate` is written correctly on login, but is reverted (usually back to `null`) milliseconds later **by the same login request**. The result is that users who sign in through `/Users/AuthenticateByName` keep showing "never logged in" in the dashboard, seemingly forever.

The sequence (verified on 10.11.11 by API testing and reading the source; the same code exists on master):

1. `SessionManager.AuthenticateNewSessionInternal` calls `UserManager.AuthenticateUser(..., isUserSession: true)`, which loads the `user` entity and then persists the login timestamps via `ExecuteUpdateAsync`:
   https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/blob/v10.11.11/Jellyfin.Server.Implementations/Users/UserManager.cs#L614-L627
   `ExecuteUpdateAsync` bypasses the EF change tracker, so the in-memory `user` entity that `AuthenticateUser` returns still carries the **old** `LastLoginDate`/`LastActivityDate` values. It also does not bump `RowVersion`.

2. Back in `AuthenticateNewSessionInternal`, that stale entity is passed to `LogSessionActivity`:
   https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/blob/v10.11.11/Emby.Server.Implementations/Session/SessionManager.cs#L1633-L1639

3. `LogSessionActivity` checks the stale `user.LastActivityDate` (old/`null`, so the "more than 60 seconds ago" guard passes), sets `user.LastActivityDate` and calls `UpdateUserAsync(user)` — a full-entity save of the stale object:
   https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/blob/v10.11.11/Emby.Server.Implementations/Session/SessionManager.cs#L263-L279
   This writes the stale `LastLoginDate` (typically `null`) back over the value step 1 just persisted. Because step 1 didn't increment `RowVersion`, the `DbUpdateConcurrencyException` catch around this save never fires.

The bug is intermittent in a misleading way: if the user was active less than 60 seconds before signing in, the guard in step 3 skips the clobbering save and `LastLoginDate` survives. So users with constantly-active sessions show plausible values while users who only log in occasionally — arguably the ones the field matters most for — permanently show "never logged in".

`LastActivityDate` is unaffected overall because the clobbering save in step 3 sets it itself.

Master has the same pattern: the `ExecuteUpdateAsync` without refreshing the tracked entity (https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/blob/master/Jellyfin.Server.Implementations/Users/UserManager.cs — `SetProperty(f => f.LastLoginDate, date)`), and the full-entity `UpdateUserAsync(user)` in `LogSessionActivity`.

### Reproduction steps

Using only the REST API against a 10.11.11 server (replace `$HOST`, `$APIKEY`):

1. Create a fresh user:
   ```bash
   curl -X POST "$HOST/Users/New?api_key=$APIKEY" -H 'content-type: application/json' \
     -d '{"Name":"lastlogin-test","Password":"test-pw-1234"}'
   ```
2. Authenticate as that user (exactly what every client does):
   ```bash
   curl -X POST "$HOST/Users/AuthenticateByName" -H 'content-type: application/json' \
     -H 'X-Emby-Authorization: MediaBrowser Client="curl", Device="curl", DeviceId="repro", Version="1.0.0"' \
     -d '{"Username":"lastlogin-test","Pw":"test-pw-1234"}'
   ```
3. Read the user back (any amount of time later):
   ```bash
   curl "$HOST/Users/{userId}?api_key=$APIKEY"
   ```

Observed: `LastLoginDate` is `null` (while `LastActivityDate` is set to the login time). Repeating step 2 and 3 never changes this. The server log shows `Authentication request for "lastlogin-test" has succeeded.` and no errors — the timestamp write happens and is then overwritten.

Expected: `LastLoginDate` reflects the successful authentication.

### Suggested fix

In `UserManager.AuthenticateUser`, after the `ExecuteUpdateAsync`, also update the returned in-memory entity:

```csharp
user.LastActivityDate = date;
user.LastLoginDate = date;
```

That keeps the entity consistent with the database, which both prevents `LogSessionActivity` from clobbering the row (its full-entity save would now carry the fresh values) and makes its 60-second guard correctly skip the redundant write during login. Happy to submit this as a PR if the approach sounds right.

### Jellyfin Server version

10.11.11 (verified); the affected code is unchanged on master.

### Environment

```
- OS: Docker (Synology NAS)
- Clients tested: raw REST API (curl / fetch), so client-independent
```

title: [Bug] STRM with local external ASS subtitle fails to burn in: file scheme is not supported
author: Ziglr
body:
### Description of the bug

When a video is provided by an HTTP URL inside a local .strm file and a local external ASS subtitle is selected for burn-in, playback fails before FFmpeg starts.

The server throws `System.NotSupportedException: The 'file' scheme is not supported.` while detecting the subtitle character set. The same failure occurs with Jellyfin iOS 1.7.0 and Litefin 1.3.0, which indicates a server-side issue.

The video plays normally when subtitles are disabled. The local ASS file is valid UTF-8 with BOM and was successfully detected as an external subtitle during the library scan.

### Reproduction steps

1. Create a local file named `video.strm` whose only content is an HTTP URL to a remote MKV file.
2. Place a valid UTF-8 with BOM ASS subtitle next to it, named `video.zh-CN.ass`.
3. Add the directory as a TV or Movies library and scan/refresh the item.
4. Confirm that Jellyfin detects the ASS file as an external Chinese subtitle.
5. Start playback with subtitles disabled; playback starts normally.
6. Select the external ASS subtitle on a client that requests subtitle burn-in, such as Jellyfin iOS.
7. Observe that playback stops and the HLS segment requests repeatedly fail before an FFmpeg process is started.

### What is the current _bug_ behavior?

Jellyfin treats the HTTP protocol of the main STRM media source as the protocol for the local subtitle while detecting its character set. It attempts to open the local `file://` subtitle through HttpClient and throws:

`System.NotSupportedException: The 'file' scheme is not supported.`

No HLS segment is produced and the client remains loading.

### What is the expected _correct_ behavior?

Jellyfin should determine the protocol from the external subtitle path independently of the main STRM media source. The local ASS subtitle should be opened as a file and passed to FFmpeg for burn-in while the video remains an HTTP input.

10.11.11

```markdown
- Reverse Proxy: None for the reproduced LAN request

- Networking: Host / LAN
- Jellyfin Data Storage & Filesystem: Local macOS filesystem
- Media Storage & Filesystem: Local .strm and local ASS sidecar; .strm points to an HTTP remote MKV
- External Integrations: None relevant
```

### Jellyfin logs

```shell

System.NotSupportedException: The 'file' scheme is not supported.
   at Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Logging.LoggingHttpMessageHandler.<SendCoreAsync>g__Core|4_0(HttpRequestMessage request, Boolean useAsync, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Logging.LoggingScopeHttpMessageHandler.<SendCoreAsync>g__Core|4_0(HttpRequestMessage request, Boolean useAsync, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at System.Net.Http.HttpClient.<SendAsync>g__Core|83_0(HttpRequestMessage request, HttpCompletionOption completionOption, CancellationTokenSource cts, Boolean disposeCts, CancellationTokenSource pendingRequestsCts, CancellationToken originalCancellationToken)
   at MediaBrowser.MediaEncoding.Subtitles.SubtitleEncoder.GetStream(String path, MediaProtocol protocol, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at MediaBrowser.MediaEncoding.Subtitles.SubtitleEncoder.GetSubtitleFileCharacterSet(MediaStream subtitleStream, String language, MediaSourceInfo mediaSource, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at MediaBrowser.Controller.MediaEncoding.EncodingHelper.GetTextSubtitlesFilter(EncodingJobInfo state, Boolean enableAlpha, Boolean enableSub2video)
   at MediaBrowser.Controller.MediaEncoding.EncodingHelper.GetAppleVidFiltersPreferred(EncodingJobInfo state, EncodingOptions options, String vidDecoder, String vidEncoder)
   at MediaBrowser.Controller.MediaEncoding.EncodingHelper.GetAppleVidFilterChain(EncodingJobInfo state, EncodingOptions options, String vidEncoder)
   at MediaBrowser.Controller.MediaEncoding.EncodingHelper.GetVideoProcessingFilterParam(EncodingJobInfo state, EncodingOptions options, String outputVideoCodec)
   at Jellyfin.Api.Controllers.DynamicHlsController.GetCommandLineArguments(String outputPath, StreamState state, Boolean isEventPlaylist, Int32 startNumber)
   at Jellyfin.Api.Controllers.DynamicHlsController.GetDynamicSegment(StreamingRequestDto streamingRequest, Int32 segmentId)
[2026-07-11 15:27:13.523 +08:00] [INF] Emby.Server.Implementations.Session.SessionManager: Playback stopped reported by app "Jellyfin iOS" "1.7.0" playing "[redacted-title]". Stopped at "unknown" ms
```

### FFmpeg logs

```shell
No FFmpeg log is generated for the failing playback attempt because the exception occurs in SubtitleEncoder.GetSubtitleFileCharacterSet before the FFmpeg command is constructed or the process is started.

For comparison, with subtitles disabled the server successfully starts FFmpeg 7.1.4-Jellyfin using the remote HTTP MKV input and `-map -0:s`.
```

### Client / Browser logs

_No response_

### Relevant screenshots or videos

_No response_

### Additional information

The source appears to contain inconsistent protocol handling.

In `SubtitleEncoder.GetReadableFile`, Jellyfin explicitly accounts for STRM files with local subtitles and derives the protocol from the subtitle path:

https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/blob/v10.11.11/MediaBrowser.MediaEncoding/Subtitles/SubtitleEncoder.cs#L249-L255

However, `GetSubtitleFileCharacterSet` opens the subtitle path using `mediaSource.Protocol`, which is HTTP for this STRM item:

https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/blob/v10.11.11/MediaBrowser.MediaEncoding/Subtitles/SubtitleEncoder.cs#L965-L978

A possible fix would be to derive the protocol from `path`, for example with `_mediaSourceManager.GetPathProtocol(path)`, instead of using the main media source protocol.

The same behavior is still present in the current master implementation of `GetSubtitleFileCharacterSet` / `DetectCharset`:
https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/blob/master/MediaBrowser.MediaEncoding/Subtitles/SubtitleEncoder.cs

author: UnendingMoo
body:
### Description of the bug

My writeup is on reddit with images, and discussion here: 
https://www.reddit.com/r/jellyfin/comments/1ut2dt0/jellyfin_creating_millions_of_photos_on_c_drive/

I will copy a partial transcript here:
"Jellyfin creating millions of photos on C drive in temp folder

So I noticed that my C: drive was unusually low on data (300gb available /1.6TB capacity) I used treesize free to find out the cause, and found that the jellyfin folder located at 'C:\Users\my\AppData\Local\Temp\jellyfin' was showing a 300+GB size.

I'm running robocopy to clean up the directory...

Here is a copy of my most recent log file if that helps: 
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1wJcwK4RGxGNykboLwUq3XN2hLn8TtsOR/view?usp=sharing
[log_20260710.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29909812/log_20260710.log)

I've included as many photos in the imgur link as I could think might relate https://imgur.com/a/FmqsXon

### Reproduction steps

I believe this post has the right idea of what's causing it:
https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/issues/15426

"

1. Create a library with some movies or TV shows
2. Set it to extract Trickplay next to media
3. Disallow write permissions on the folder (-rw-r--r-- user:user should do)
4. Start extract job

"

### What is the current _bug_ behavior?

Again see: https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/issues/15426
Copying here for posterity

1. "Jellyfin begins Trickplay extraction in the cache folder

- [i]     Extracts frames to /tmp/jellyfin/[id]...
- [ii]     Combines frames in /tmp/jellyfin/trickplay_[id]...
- [iii]     Cleans up initial frames folder: /tmp/jellyfin/[id]...

2. Jellyfin attempts to create the target folder, but fails due to missing permissions

    - [i]     Eg /home/user/Movies/Tron (2025)/Tron (2025).trickplay/

3. Aborts without cleaning the final /tmp/jellyfin/trickplay_[id]... folder
"

### What is the expected _correct_ behavior?

Again: https://github.com/jellyfin/jellyfin/issues/15426

"
One of the following:

1.     Trickplay images are generated in-place, requiring that the target trickplay folder exist and be writable before FFmpeg even starts.
2.     At least check that the target folder exists and is writable before starting the Trickplay extraction in the cache folder. This prevents unnecessary work.
3.     Simply clean up the Trickplay folder if it fails to copy.

body: ### Description of the bug Same issue as #8446. When playing a video using Direct Streaming, I can switch the audio track correctly. However, below a certain streaming quality (under 4 Mbps), the English audio track stops working. This happens consistently with this episode when the streaming quality is set below 4 Mbps. ### Reproduction steps 1. Start playing a video using Direct Streaming. 2. Switch to the English audio track. 3. Lower the streaming quality to below 4 Mbps. 4. The English audio track no longer produces any sound (or stops working). ### What is the current _bug_ behavior? Same issue as #8446. When playing a video using Direct Streaming, I can switch the audio track correctly. However, below a certain streaming quality (under 4 Mbps), the English audio track stops working. This happens consistently with this episode when the streaming quality is set below 4 Mbps. ### What is the expected _correct_ behavior? The selected audio track should continue to work correctly regardless of the streaming quality, including below 4 Mbps. ### Jellyfin Server version 10.11.11 ### Specify commit id _No response_ ### title: Trickplay generation runs away indefinitely on files with corrupted Duration metadata
author: mels0n
body:
### Description of the bug

If a video file's container reports an implausible Duration value (wildly disagreeing with its own real frame count and actual playable length), "Generate Trickplay Images" does not detect or reject this. It runs away, generating vastly more frame images than the real video length could ever justify, and never completes on its own. Observed: one ~42-minute episode produced 60,000+ trickplay frame JPEGs (implying ~169 hours of "content") before being manually cancelled, still climbing when killed.

### Reproduction steps

1. Have a video file whose container reports an implausible Duration (e.g. years) that wildly disagrees with its real frame count and actual playable length.
2. Trigger trickplay generation for that item (library scan, or manually via the "Generate Trickplay Images" scheduled task).
3. Observe: trickplay generation for that item never completes and continues writing frame images to the temp directory indefinitely (observed: 60,000+ frames and still climbing before being manually killed).

### What is the current _bug_ behavior?

The file had a corrupted `Duration` value in its container metadata (confirmed independently of Jellyfin, via `ffprobe -show_format -show_streams` run directly on the file):
- `duration=322843082.752000` (seconds), implying ~3,736 days (~10.2 years)
- `TAG:DURATION=89678:38:15.485000000`, the same absurd value, embedded in the container itself
- `TAG:NUMBER_OF_FRAMES=60936`
- `r_frame_rate=24/1`, `avg_frame_rate=24/1` (confirmed constant frame rate, not VFR)

60,936 frames at a real 24fps CFR = 2,539 seconds ≈ 42.3 minutes, a completely normal episode. The real video content and per-frame timing were fine. Only the container's stated Duration was corrupted (the corruption itself originates outside Jellyfin, from a third-party muxing tool, but Jellyfin's handling of it is the actual bug being reported here).

Jellyfin's own `RunTimeTicks` for this item (`GET /Items/{id}`) matched the corrupted value exactly, confirming the library scan trusted the corrupted container metadata with no sanity check against the file's own frame count or actual stream length.

When "Generate Trickplay Images" then ran on this file, the `fps=0.1` sampling filter used to extract one trickplay frame per 10 seconds did not correctly decimate to that rate. Instead of respecting the real ~24fps native frame timing, it emitted close to one output frame per real decoded frame. This is consistent with its internal time-pacing being computed against the corrupted multi-year duration rather than the real, sane per-frame timestamps.

- The task hangs on this item indefinitely (severity depends on the "Scan Behavior" setting), making no real progress toward completion.
- Cancelling the task does not reliably clean up the temp directory afterward (see #15426 below, which compounds this): a single file with this kind of corrupted metadata can accumulate an effectively unbounded number of orphaned temp files on disk before anyone notices.

### What is the expected _correct_ behavior?

- Sanity-check a file's Duration against its own frame count and frame rate (or an independently-computed stream duration) before trusting it for trickplay frame-pacing. Fall back to a computed duration when the two disagree significantly.
- Regardless of root cause, cap trickplay frame generation at a sane hard limit (e.g. expected-duration/interval plus generous headroom) so a file with corrupted metadata fails cleanly and visibly instead of running away indefinitely.
- Ensure a cancelled or failed trickplay job's temp directory is always cleaned up (see #15426).

### Jellyfin Server version

10.11.10

_No response_

_No response_

10.11.10

```markdown
- FFmpeg 7.1.3 (jellyfin-ffmpeg)
- Hardware acceleration: Intel QSV/VAAPI
- Trickplay settings: Image Interval 10000ms (0.1 fps), Width 320, hardware-accelerated MJPEG encoding enabled
```

title: Library scan leaves episodes/seasons with stale SeriesPresentationUniqueKey after series identification — series browsable but seasons/episodes invisible (root cause + SQL detection + repro for #16097 family)
author: jakevolkman
body:
### Description of the bug

On 10.11.x, when a library scan ingests a new TV series and the series is subsequently identified against a metadata provider (TVDB in my case), the series item's PresentationUniqueKey is rewritten from its initial raw-id form (e.g. 95267af313b22249ae646cf9cc6b066b) to the provider-based form (e.g. 73141-en-a656b907eb3a73532e40e44b968d0225), but the already-created child seasons and episodes keep the old key in SeriesPresentationUniqueKey.

Season/episode hierarchy queries (/Shows/{id}/Seasons, /Shows/{id}/Episodes) join on that key, so affected series show up in the library but return zero seasons and zero episodes:

Android TV app: series tile visible, detail page has no episodes and no play button at all.
Web: series visible; episodes reachable through some non-hierarchical paths (search, Episodes tab), which makes the symptom look intermittent/random.
The items are fully probed and playable — MediaStreamInfos rows exist and PlaybackInfo on an affected episode (by id) returns a valid MediaSource. Only the hierarchy linkage is broken.
I believe this is the underlying mechanism for the "Can't Reproduce" cluster: #16097 (new seasons not displaying), #15040 (seasons became virtual/empty), #16093 (folder appears, no episodes), discussion #16326 (Shows libraries broken since 10.11.x), and plausibly #15200 (Continue Watching queries also filter on SeriesPresentationUniqueKey).

It reproduces on every ingest of new series, not just upgrades: this was a fresh 10.11.11 install (empty database, no migration from 10.10). The initial scan of an 11-series library broke 7 of them; a later copy of ~6 new kids' shows into the library broke every one of the new series the same way.

Likely-duplicate issues to reference: #16097, #15040, #16093, #15200, discussion #16326.

Detection (SQL):
Against jellyfin.db (WAL — copy the -wal alongside or open live):

SELECT s.Name,
       s.PresentationUniqueKey AS series_key,
       (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM BaseItems c
         WHERE c.SeriesId = s.Id
           AND c.Type LIKE '%Episode%'
           AND c.SeriesPresentationUniqueKey <> s.PresentationUniqueKey) AS stale_children
FROM BaseItems s
WHERE s.Type LIKE '%TV.Series%';
Healthy series: stale_children = 0. Affected series: all children stale, and the stale value equals the series' item id (its pre-identification key). Example from my library:

American Dad!  series key: 73141-en-a656b907eb3a73532e40e44b968d0225
   episodes'   SeriesPresentationUniqueKey: 95267af313b22249ae646cf9cc6b066b  (= the series' own Id)

### Reproduction steps

Fresh 10.11.11 server, one "Shows" library.
Copy a new series into the library root (standard Show Name/Season 01/Show S01E01.mkv layout) — in my case media lives on a read-only NFS mount, but I don't believe that's material.
Let the scheduled/triggered library scan ingest and identify it (TVDB match succeeds).
Run the SQL above → children keyed to the pre-identification key; /Shows/{seriesId}/Seasons returns TotalRecordCount: 0 for any user (admin included).
Observed repeatedly: initial library scan (7/11 series affected) and every subsequent new-series ingest (6/6 series affected, across two batches).
Possibly relevant timing detail: the affected batches were scanned while files were still being copied in / shortly after; the scan may be racing identification. Two shows also ended up with duplicate series records (same provider key) from the same scans.

### What is the current _bug_ behavior?

Season/episode hierarchy queries (/Shows/{id}/Seasons, /Shows/{id}/Episodes) join on that key, so affected series show up in the library but return zero seasons and zero episodes:

Android TV app: series tile visible, detail page has no episodes and no play button at all.
Web: series visible; episodes reachable through some non-hierarchical paths (search, Episodes tab), which makes the symptom look intermittent/random.
The items are fully probed and playable — MediaStreamInfos rows exist and PlaybackInfo on an affected episode (by id) returns a valid MediaSource. Only the hierarchy linkage is broken.

### What is the expected _correct_ behavior?

All series show up in the library including all seasons and all episodes.

### Jellyfin Server version

10.11.11

### Specify commit id

_No response_

### Specify unstable release number

_No response_

### Specify version number

_No response_

### Specify the build version

10.11.11

### Environment

```markdown
- Jellyfin 10.11.11+deb13 (official apt repo), fresh install — not an upgrade/migration
- Debian 13 (trixie), unprivileged LXC on Proxmox VE 9.2; media on read-only NFS mount
- Clients where observed: Android TV app 0.19.9, Web (Chrome 150), plus raw API
- Metadata providers: defaults (TVDB/TMDB), English
```

### Jellyfin logs

```shell
I'll report my implemented fix in this box:

Per-series POST /Items/{seriesId}/Refresh?Recursive=true&MetadataRefreshMode=FullRefresh&ImageRefreshMode=Default&ReplaceAllMetadata=false&ReplaceAllImages=false rewrites the children's SeriesPresentationUniqueKey; the SQL then reports 0 stale children and seasons/episodes reappear on all clients. I currently run a cron that detects mismatches with the SQL above and queues that refresh — 100% effective so far, which is why I'm fairly confident about the mechanism.
```

### FFmpeg logs

```shell

```

### Client / Browser logs

_No response_

### Relevant screenshots or videos

_No response_

### Additional information

I encountered this bug after troubleshooting it for too long; Fable 5 fixed it and gave me the above outputs to send to you for the bug report because when I asked Fable if the Jellyfin devs were aware of this bug, it looked through your issues and found that it couldn't be reproduced yet. Hope this helps.
